setwd("C:/Users/amperez/Desktop/surv")

insurance <- read.table("insurance.txt", header=TRUE, dec=".")

attach(insurance)

#1 fit a AFT model with weibull distribution

#library(survival)
srFitE1 <-survreg(Surv(duration,delta,type="right")~poverty+smoke+alcohol+agemth+yschool, data=insurance, dist="exponential")
summary(srFitE1)

accf<-exp(-srFitE1$coefficients[3])
accf

xb<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=1,alcohol=0,agemth=0,yschool=0),type="lp")
e_rate=1/exp(xb)
pexp(20,rate=e_rate, lower.tail = F)

xb0<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=0,alcohol=0,agemth=0,yschool=0),type="lp")
e_rate=1/exp(xb0)
pexp(20*accf,rate=e_rate, lower.tail = F)

#another way how to check it

s13<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=1,alcohol=0,agemth=0,yschool=0),type="quantile",p=1-0.133959)
s13

s0_13<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=0,alcohol=0,agemth=0,yschool=0),type="quantile",p=1-0.133959)
s0_13

s0_13/s13 #equals the acceleration factor

#check expectation

E_expo<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=1,alcohol=1,agemth=40,yschool=16),type="response")
E_expo

xb<-predict(srFitE1, newdata=data.frame(poverty=0,smoke=1,alcohol=1,agemth=40,yschool=16),type="lp")
e_rate=1/exp(xb)
E2_expo=1/e_rate
E2_expo
